creator: fabian@wenks.ch
text:
(In reply to Adam Weinberger from comment #3)

I am also building geoipupdate since a long time and this was never an issue.
As far as I see it, in the previous versions of geoipupdate the line 'toolchain go1.22.3' (or similar version) was never present and started with 7.1.0 as you can see with this start of the diff:

riddler:/usr/ports/ # diff -u distfiles/go/net_geoipupdate/geoipupdate-v7.0.1/v7.0.1.mod distfiles/go/net_geoipupdate/geoipupdate-v7.1.0/v7.1.0.mod
--- distfiles/go/net_geoipupdate/geoipupdate-v7.0.1/v7.0.1.mod	2024-04-08 23:36:08.000000000 +0200
+++ distfiles/go/net_geoipupdate/geoipupdate-v7.1.0/v7.1.0.mod	2024-11-18 18:59:35.000000000 +0100
@@ -1,24 +1,25 @@
 module github.com/maxmind/geoipupdate/v7
 
-go 1.20
+go 1.21.0
 
+toolchain go1.22.3
+
 require (
 	github.com/cenkalti/backoff/v4 v4.3.0


Did also check for 'toolchain' in all files I have still around, and only v7.1.0 does match:

riddler:/usr/ports/ # ls -tr1 distfiles/go/net_geoipupdate/geoipupdate-v*/v*mod
distfiles/go/net_geoipupdate/geoipupdate-v4.8.0/v4.8.0.mod
distfiles/go/net_geoipupdate/geoipupdate-v4.9.0/v4.9.0.mod
distfiles/go/net_geoipupdate/geoipupdate-v4.10.0/v4.10.0.mod
distfiles/go/net_geoipupdate/geoipupdate-v4.11.0/v4.11.0.mod
distfiles/go/net_geoipupdate/geoipupdate-v4.11.1/v4.11.1.mod
distfiles/go/net_geoipupdate/geoipupdate-v5.0.2/v5.0.2.mod
distfiles/go/net_geoipupdate/geoipupdate-v5.0.4/v5.0.4.mod
distfiles/go/net_geoipupdate/geoipupdate-v5.1.1/v5.1.1.mod
distfiles/go/net_geoipupdate/geoipupdate-v6.0.0/v6.0.0.mod
distfiles/go/net_geoipupdate/geoipupdate-v6.1.0/v6.1.0.mod
distfiles/go/net_geoipupdate/geoipupdate-v7.0.1/v7.0.1.mod
distfiles/go/net_geoipupdate/geoipupdate-v7.1.0/v7.1.0.mod
riddler:/usr/ports/ # grep toolchain distfiles/go/net_geoipupdate/geoipupdate-v*/v*mod 
distfiles/go/net_geoipupdate/geoipupdate-v7.1.0/v7.1.0.mod:toolchain go1.22.3
riddler:/usr/ports/ # 


Looks like this is go internal hint on what to use to build this go application with, and the Port build does just use it. Maybe some other go based Ports have the same issue as well, but as probably distfiles most often is on a filesystem which allows exec, nobody did recognize that so far.

I currently use only one other go application which is security/lego, but checking their v*.mod files do not have 'toolchain' in it.
